content: Newton-Raphson IV solvers fail near-zero-vega regions (deep ITM/OTM, near expiry) causing large swings or divergence; bisection is the standard robust fallback (guaranteed convergence via bracketing) but is slow and still garbage-in-garbage-out on illiquid quotes - using raw bid/ask instead of mid, or pricing wide-spread/zero-volume strikes, produces biased/unstable IV; deep ITM prices are dominated by intrinsic value leaving too little time-value signal to invert. Practitioner recommendation: hybrid Newton-with-bisection-fallback, put-call parity conversion for stability, and explicit vega-floor / max-iteration guards that surface a solver-failed state rather than returning a garbage IV.
